jobs.company: University Medical Center of El Paso (UMC)
jobs.title: Program Director Burn
jobs.description:
Summary

Job Summary

The Burn Program Director provides leadership, strategic direction, and administrative oversight of the burn program. This role is responsible for the development, growth, and sustainability of the burn center, ensuring compliance with American Burn Association (ABA) verification standards and alignment with organizational strategic priorities. Serves as the primary liaison between hospital executive leadership and the burn medical director, leading multidisciplinary collaboration across the continuum of care and ensuring clinical quality, operational excellence, financial stewardship, and regulatory compliance. Responsible for education, staff (direct reports and clinical oversight for those who provide burn care), inventory, resources, knowledge management, and multidisciplinary collaboration.

Minimum Job Requirements

Work Experience

Three years in program leadership (burn, trauma, or critical care. Demonstrated experience in program development, accreditation, and performance improvement.

Five years of progressive management clinical and operational experience required.

License/Registration/Certification

Current Texas Registered Nurse (RN) license or Compact RN license required.

Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required.

Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) and Advanced Burn Life Support (ABLS) certifications required; may be obtained within one year of placement in the position.

Certified Burn Registered Nurse (CBRN) certification through the Board of Certification for Emergency Nursing (BCEN) required; may be obtained within two years of placement in the position.

Trauma Nursing Core Course (TNCC) or Advanced Trauma Care for Nurses (ATCN) certification required; may be obtained within one year of placement in the position.

Education and Training

Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) required.

Master’s degree in Nursing, Healthcare Administration, or Business Administration preferred.

Skills

Advanced knowledge of burn care, nursing practice standards, and factors affecting the delivery of patient care services.
Ability to lead organizational changes and strategic initiatives that improve patient care, clinical outcomes, and nursing practice.
Knowledge of regulatory, accreditation, and verification standards applicable to burn programs and healthcare operations.
Strong leadership skills with the ability to build high-performing teams and promote collaboration, accountability, and shared goals.
Well-developed verbal, written, presentation, and interpersonal communication skills.
Proven ability to negotiate, mediate, and build consensus among physicians, leadership, staff, and external stakeholders.
Strong analytical, numerical, critical thinking, and problem-solving abilities.
Ability to develop, implement, and evaluate strategic plans, operational initiatives, and program growth opportunities.
Knowledge of performance improvement, quality management, patient safety, and outcome measurement methodologies.
Ability to effectively manage budgets, financial resources, capital planning, and operational performance.
Demonstrated teaching, coaching, mentoring, presentation, and educational program development skills.
Ability to lead multidisciplinary teams, coordinate care across the continuum, and facilitate effective patient progression and discharge planning.
Ability to plan and successfully execute projects and change initiatives of significant scope and complexity.
Ability to cultivate and maintain productive relationships with community agencies, referral sources, professional organizations, and healthcare partners.
Ability to serve as a clinical expert and role model while fostering a culture of excellence, innovation, customer service, and continuous improvement.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Hospitals and Health Care

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Job Details

Description

Tacodeli is looking for a hands-on Director of Operations to lead our restaurants with heart, hustle, and a passion for great food. Reporting to the CEO, you’ll drive exceptional guest experiences, strong restaurant performance, and a culture that reflects what makes Austin special.

What You’ll Do

Lead and coach District Managers, Culinary and restaurant managers to deliver top-tier hospitality and consistent food quality
Drive operational excellence across all locations
Turn strategy into clear plans and hold teams accountable to results (labor, food cost, guest experience)
Build strong teams by developing leaders and attracting great talent
Partner with Finance and cross-functional teams to improve performance and processes
Regularly spend time in restaurants, observing service, coaching teams, and ensuring operational excellence.

What You Bring

Multi-unit restaurant leadership experience
Strong people leadership and coaching skills
Solid understanding of restaurant operations and financials
Hands-on, team-first leadership style
Passion for hospitality, food, and tacos

Why Tacodeli

People-first culture
Growing brand with deep community roots
Opportunity to make a real impact

Tacodeli is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Management and Manufacturing

Industries

Restaurants
